name: Rabbit, Mexican Style
recipeIngredient: 1 can Condensed chicken broth (10-1/2 ounce can)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the legs from the rabbits and cut the backs into three pieces.
2. In a Dutch oven, heat oil and add chili peppers. Fry until brown, crushing to extract juice. Remove peppers.
3. Rub rabbit pieces with garlic and coat with flour. Brown rabbit pieces in the oil on all sides. Cover and simmer until tender (about 1 to 1-1/2 hours).
4. Pour off excess fat.
5. Melt butter in a large saucepan. Saute carrot, onion, green pepper, and mushrooms until vegetables are soft.
6. Stir in flour, then gradually stir in chicken broth and orange juice. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens slightly.
7. Add orange peel, peanut butter, cumin seeds, toasted sesame seeds, cloves, nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste. Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Place rabbit pieces on a platter. Skim excess fat from the sauce surface.
9. Spoon sauce over the rabbit pieces and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
